title: Host Hotels & Resorts: Inflationary Pressures Could Cause Headwinds
text: Host Hotels & Resorts, Inc. has seen a strong recovery in overall RevPAR. However, revenue growth could increasingly plateau in the face of higher prices.

title: Risks Of 'No Landing'
text: U.S. equity markets were mixed on a choppy week as investors readjusted expectations for inflation and interest rates following data indicating an uptick in economic activity and persisting price pressures. Posting its first back-to-back weekly declines of the year, the S&P 500 slipped 0.2% this week, but other major benchmarks fared better. The Mid-Cap 400 and Small-Cap 600 both gained.

title: Host Hotels & Resorts Provides Updated Fourth Quarter 2022 Investor Presentation
text: BETHESDA, Md., Feb. 17, 2023 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Host Hotels & Resorts, Inc. (NASDAQ: HST), the nation's largest lodging real estate investment trust (the "Company"), today provided an updated investor presentation for fourth quarter 2022 results. The investor presentation can be found on the Investor Relations section on the company's website at https://www.hosthotels.com/investor-relations/investor-presentation.

title: Host Hotels & Resorts, Inc. (HST) Q4 2022 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Host Hotels & Resorts, Inc. (HST) Q4 2022 Earnings Conference Call February 16, 2023 10:00 AM ET Corporate Participants Jaime Marcus - SVP, Investor Relations Jim Risoleo - President and Chief Executive Officer Sourav Ghosh - EVP and Chief Financial Officer Conference Call Participants Smedes Rose - Citigroup Anthony Powell - Barclays David Katz - Jefferies Chris Darling - Green Street Chris Woronka - Deutsche Bank Duane Pfennigwerth - Evercore ISI Ari Klein - BMO Robin Farley - UBS Jay Kornreich - SMBC Floris van Dijkum - Compass Point Operator Good morning, and welcome to the Host Hotels & Resorts Fourth Quarter 2022 Earnings Conference Call. Today's conference is being recorded.

title: Host Hotels' (HST) FFO & Revenues Surpass Estimates in Q4
text: Host Hotels' (HST) Q4 results outshine estimates. The quarterly performance was aided by continued strong leisure travel demand and growth in the urban markets.

title: Host Hotels (HST) Q4 FFO and Revenues Beat Estimates
text: Host Hotels (HST) delivered FFO and revenue surprises of 2.33% and 1.24%, respectively, for the quarter ended December 2022. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?

title: Host Hotels & Resorts, Inc. Reports Results for 2022; RevPAR Exceeds 2019 for Third Consecutive Quarter; Full Year Performance Finishes at High End of Q3 Guidance
text: BETHESDA, Md., Feb. 15, 2023 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Host Hotels & Resorts, Inc. (NASDAQ: HST) (the “Company”), the nation's largest lodging real estate investment trust (“REIT”), today announced results for fourth quarter and full year 2022.
